perturbation.original_text: The parties to this Agreement have agreed as follows: 1. Non-Disclosure. Recipient agrees as follows: 1.1. For a period of three (3) years from the date of entering in to this Agreement, not to disclosure any Information or Item, in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, whether in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party, to any individual, firm or entity, excepting solely an individual <PAGE> 2 who (a) is an officer, director or employee of Disclosing Party or of the Recipient and (b) has a clear need to know for purposes directly related to the Matter.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties to this Agreement have agreed as follows: 1. Non-Disclosure. Recipient agrees as follows: 1.1. Not to disclosure any Information or Item, in any manner whatsoever, directly or indirectly, whether in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party, to any individual, firm or entity, excepting solely an individual <PAGE> 2 who (a) is an officer, director or employee of Disclosing Party or of the Recipient and (b) has a clear need to know for purposes directly related to the Matter.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'For a period of three (3) years from the date of entering in to this Agreement' has been removed from Section 1.1. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding how long non-disclosure obligations last, because if Section 6 refers to two years, and Section 1.1 lacks defined time limit, enforcement of non-disclosure becomes uncertain because it is not clearly indicated how long this agreement is viable for, in comparison with the employee period that is well defined in Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of two (2) years Recipient (or any agent or affiliates of the Recipient) will not have discussions with any "Covered Employees" of the Disclosing Party regarding employment opportunities with the Recipient or any of its affiliates. "Covered Employees" will include any person who is employed by the recipient as a part or full time employee either as of the date of this Agreement, or persons that subsequently become employees of the Disclosing Party. However, if a person subsequently terminates their employment with the Disclosing Party, then the recipient can have such discussions with that person, after a 5-day waiting period. In addition, if the Recipient published a general posting for employment opportunities in any publication or web site, and one of the Recipient's Covered Employees should respond to the posting, then the Recipient will be free to have such discussions with that person.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 1.1 removes the 'three (3) years' time limit for non-disclosure, while the contradicted text in another section specifies a 'two (2) years' limit for employee discussions. The removal of the time limit in Section 1.1 creates a contradiction because now the agreement has two sections specifying different time periods for obligations under the contract. This introduces uncertainty about how long non-disclosure obligations last and therefore results in a direct conflict within the document, aligning with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Exclusions. The obligations of a Recipient under Section 1.1 hereof shall not apply to the following: 2.1. Any Information which, at the time of disclosure, is already rightfully possessed by Recipient or is already in the public domain; 2.2. Any Information which, after the disclosure, enters the public domain without any action or fault of Recipient. 2.3. Any Information which is obtained, other than in connection with the Matter, from any individual, firm or entity which had the unrestricted right to disclose it; 2.4. Any Information which is developed independently by Recipient without reference to any Information or Item;
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Exclusions. The obligations of a Recipient under Section 1.1 hereof shall not apply to the following: 2.1. Any Information which, at the time of disclosure, is already rightfully possessed by Recipient or is already in the public domain; 2.2. Any Information which, after the disclosure, enters the public domain without any action or fault of Recipient;
perturbation.explanation: Exclusions 2.3 and 2.4 has been removed from Section 2. By omitting `2.3. Any Information which is obtained, other than in connection with the Matter, from any individual, firm or entity which had the unrestricted right to disclose it; 2.4. Any Information which is developed independently by Recipient without reference to any Information or Item;`, the exclusion criteria is drastically reduced. This omission creates an internal contradiction because Section 5 states `Only One Source of Discussion`, which states the recipient can only discuss the matter with Mr. Al Weber, Chief Executive Officer, or Mr. Thomas V. Gilboy, Chief Financial Officer of the Disclosing Party. Removing the Exclusions clause 2.3 and 2.4 now the recipient can be liable to discussing the information with third parties, and the agreement will still be valid due to Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree that you will only discuss the Matter with Mr. Al Weber, Chief Executive Officer, or Mr. Thomas V. Gilboy, Chief Financial Officer of the Disclosing Party. Under no circumstances will you discuss the Matter, or admit that the Matter has been a topic of discussion with any other employees of the Disclosing Party, or any other third party. In addition, during 2000, you agree not to undertake any unusual efforts to research the business, operations, prospects or finances of the Disclosing Party, where such research efforts would include discussions with any third party, except in the ordinary course of business.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 2 included exclusions 2.3 and 2.4, which allowed the Recipient to disclose information if obtained from a third party with the unrestricted right to disclose it, or if independently developed. The changed text removes these exclusions. The contradicted text in Section 5 strictly limits discussion of the Matter to specific individuals within the Disclosing Party and prohibits discussion with any other third party. By removing exclusions 2.3 and 2.4, the changed text now contradicts Section 5 because the Recipient would be in violation of the agreement (Section 5) by obtaining/developing information covered in 2.3 and 2.4, whereas it would have been permitted under the original text.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Only One Source of Discussion. You agree that you will only discuss the Matter with Mr. Al Weber, Chief Executive Officer, or Mr. Thomas V. Gilboy, Chief Financial Officer of the Disclosing Party. Under no circumstances will you discuss the Matter, or admit that the Matter has been a topic of discussion with any other employees of the Disclosing Party, or any other third party. In addition, during 2000, you agree not to undertake any unusual efforts to research the business, operations, prospects or finances of the Disclosing Party, where such research efforts would include discussions with any third party, except in the ordinary course of business.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Only One Source of Discussion. You agree that you will only discuss the Matter with Mr. Al Weber, Chief Executive Officer, or Mr. Thomas V. Gilboy, Chief Financial Officer of the Disclosing Party. Under no circumstances will you discuss the Matter, or admit that the Matter has been a topic of discussion with any other employees of the Disclosing Party, or any other third party.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'In addition, during 2000, you agree not to undertake any unusual efforts to research the business, operations, prospects or finances of the Disclosing Party, where such research efforts would include discussions with any third party, except in the ordinary course of business.' has been removed from Section 5. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 2.4 states that the contract will not apply to `Any Information which is developed independently by Recipient without reference to any Information or Item`. By omitting the phrase, the Recipient can freely research the business without the limitations, and exclusion 2.4 in section 2 still holds, thus the contract and its restrictions will not apply to the recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Exclusions. The obligations of a Recipient under Section 1.1 hereof shall not apply to the following: 2.1. Any Information which, at the time of disclosure, is already rightfully possessed by Recipient or is already in the public domain; 2.2. Any Information which, after the disclosure, enters the public domain without any action or fault of Recipient. 2.3. Any Information which is obtained, other than in connection with the Matter, from any individual, firm or entity which had the unrestricted right to disclose it; 2.4. Any Information which is developed independently by Recipient without reference to any Information or Item;
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the recipient's ability to research the disclosing party during 2000. The changed text removes this limitation. Section 2.4 excludes information developed independently by the recipient without reference to the disclosed information from the obligations of the agreement. Because the recipient is now free to research the Disclosing Party without limitation, this research can be claimed to be `developed independently` as the phrase was removed, thereby allowing the recipient to circumvent the restrictions of Section 1.1, which is directly contradicted.

perturbation.original_text: 9. No Proprietary Right; Return of Information or Items. Nothing herein shall confer upon Recipient any right, title, interest or license whatsoever in or to any Information or Items. At the end of our discussions regarding the Matter, the Disclosing Party has the right to request that the Recipient return all Information or Items to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. No Proprietary Right; Return of Information or Items. Nothing herein shall confer upon Recipient any right, title, interest or license whatsoever in or to any Information or Items.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'At the end of our discussions regarding the Matter, the Disclosing Party has the right to request that the Recipient return all Information or Items to the Disclosing Party.' has been removed from Section 9. This means the obligation to return the Information or Items has been removed, creating a conflict with Section 8. Preservation of markings, where there is a clause that states `Recipient shall not alter or delete any markings on any Information or Item restricting its disclosure, use or reproduction or indicating its proprietary or private status.`. However now that the obligation to return the item has been removed, it does not make sense to preserve the markings on a item that may or may not be returned. This creates an in-text contradiction because without the return requirement, the continued prohibition on altering markings becomes questionable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall not alter or delete any markings on any Information or Item restricting its disclosure, use or reproduction or indicating its proprietary or private status. Recipient shall include all such markings in all copies of any Information which it makes.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a requirement for the Recipient to return all Information or Items to the Disclosing Party at the end of discussions. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text states, 'Recipient shall not alter or delete any markings on any Information or Item...'. By removing the obligation to return the item, the purpose of preserving the markings becomes unclear and creates a logical inconsistency, since the recipient may now retain the item indefinitely. The absence of a return obligation directly contradicts the clause about preserving markings, making it a strong in-text contradiction as it questions the need to preserve markings on an item they might keep.
